title: I Wish I Knew That Woman's Name,
content:
I wish I knew that woman's name,
    So, when she comes this way,
    To hold my life, and hold my ears,
    For fear I hear her say

    She's 'sorry I am dead,' again,
    Just when the grave and I
    Have sobbed ourselves almost to sleep, --
    Our only lullaby.
meaning:
Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "I Wish I Knew That Woman's Name,"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
